price_correlation.highest_price_title: Tudor Black Bay GMT 79830RB Black Dial Steel Watch
price_correlation.note: The single highest sale ($4,584) used a sparse title with only 6 keywords — no 'Pepsi', no '41mm', no 'Automatic'. However, this is one data point and may reflect factors unrelated to title (e.g., seller reputation, condition). The mid-range keyword-rich titles ($3,500–$3,745) show consistent clustering. Content skill should optimize for discoverability (keyword-rich) rather than mimicking the single highest sale's sparse format.

validation_evidence: eBay autocomplete: 'tudor gmt pepsi', 'tudor black bay gmt pepsi', 'tudor gmt pepsi opaline'
eBay sold comps: appears in 6 of 7 used titles
Google organic: appears in 4 of 6 result titles

dial_variant_filter.reason: Opaline describes a different dial variant (M79830RB-0010/0012) of the same reference. Including it in the title for a black dial watch is factually inaccurate and would mislead buyers. 'White' also filtered as synonym for Opaline in Chrono24 titles.

case_size_validation.conflicting_value_source: eBay sold comp: 'Tudor Black Bay GMT 79830RB Pepsi 42mm Steel Black Dial Automatic Men's Watch'
case_size_validation.resolution: Use 41mm from input data. 42mm in that competitor title is incorrect for the 79830RB reference (spec is 41mm). Do not use 42mm in recommended keywords.

completeness_filter.note: Input data confirms Full Set. These keywords are valid for Priority 3 inclusion and the content skill may use them.

recommended_title_keywords.reference_placement: Item Specifics / structured fields only, not in title

competitor_gap.note: Most competitor titles on eBay and Chrono24 lead with the reference number or use minimal descriptor keywords. A retail-keyword-forward title ('Tudor Black Bay GMT Pepsi 41mm Automatic Men's Watch Steel') would surface on non-reference queries — 'tudor pepsi gmt watch', 'tudor automatic 41mm steel', 'tudor black bay gmt mens' — that reference-heavy titles miss entirely. The black dial is also a differentiator worth calling out explicitly since many comps default to Opaline.
